extension.description: 2100+ Dart/Flutter lint rules with visual analysis, plus Package Vibrancy for dependency health scoring, vulnerability scanning, and upgrade planning.

viewsWelcome.banner.contents:
**Saropa Lints**

2100+ Dart and Flutter lint rules for security, accessibility, and performance.

Requires a Dart or Flutter project.

Open a folder containing a `pubspec.yaml` to get started.

[Learn more online](https://pub.dev/packages/saropa_lints)
[Browse All Commands](command:saropaLints.showCommandCatalog)
[About Saropa Lints](command:saropaLints.showAbout)
[Getting Started](command:saropaLints.openWalkthrough)

config.property.enabled.markdownDescript: When **on** (default), Saropa Lints runs upgrade checks and treats the workspace as using Saropa lint integration. Turn **off** to pause integration notifications and status-bar score without hiding sidebar options—use **Saropa Lints: Set Up Project** when you need to add `saropa_lints` to pubspec and write `analysis_options`.
config.property.tier.description: Default tier when enabling or re-initializing.
config.property.runAnalysisAfterConfigCh: Run dart analyze after init or config change when enabled.
config.property.uiLanguage.description: UI language for Saropa sidebar and dashboards. Use 'auto' to follow VS Code display language. The sidebar language picker saves this value to User settings so it applies in every workspace.
config.property.runAnalysisOpenEditorsOn: When true, Saropa Lints runs dart analyze only for Dart files currently open in VS Code (workspace.textDocuments) under the detected project root.
config.property.inlineAnnotations.descri: Show violation messages at the end of affected lines (Error Lens style).
config.property.issuesPageSize.descripti: Maximum number of violations shown per file in grouped findings (Findings Dashboard / internal list). Remaining are shown as "and N more…".
config.property.violationsGroupBy.descri: Default grouping for lint findings (Findings Dashboard / grouped list). "impact" lists Critical and High first; use the dashboard toolbar to change temporarily.
config.property.checkForUpdates.descript: Periodically check for newer versions of the saropa_lints package and offer to upgrade.
config.property.pubspecValidation.prefer: When **on**, pubspec validation prefers exact version pins (`1.2.3`) and flags caret ranges (`^1.2.3`). When **off** (default), it prefers caret syntax and flags bare versions — matching `dart pub add` behavior. Only one mode is active at a time.
config.section.Code.Health.title: Code Health
config.property.projectVibrancy.lcovPath: LCOV file path for **Code Health** scans from the dashboard (relative to the project root, or absolute). Same as CLI `--lcov` — configure here so you never rely on CLI-only runs. Setting key kept as `projectVibrancy.*` so existing user settings keep working after the dashboard was renamed from Project Vibrancy.
config.property.projectVibrancy.minGrade: Minimum average grade (A=best). F disables this gate for extension scans. Non-zero gate failures still show a banner and copyable JSON.
config.property.projectVibrancy.maxUnuse: Fail the scan when unused function count exceeds this value. **0** = do not apply this gate (same as omitting `--max-unused`).
config.property.projectVibrancy.maxUncov: Fail when uncovered function count exceeds this value. **0** = off.
config.property.projectVibrancy.maxStubT: Fail when `stub_tested` function count exceeds this value. **0** = off.
config.property.projectVibrancy.maxSuspi: Fail when `suspicious_coverage` function count exceeds this value. **0** = off.
config.property.projectVibrancy.maxTestD: Fail when `test_drift` function count exceeds this value. **0** = off.
config.section.Activity.bar.title: Activity bar
config.property.sidebar.showOverview.des: Show the **Saropa Lints** sidebar (flat list of editor dashboards, actions, status rows, settings, triage, and help links). Detailed Summary, File Risk, Package Vibrancy, and Drift dashboards live in editor tabs.
config.section.TODOs.Hacks.title: TODOs & Hacks
config.property.todosAndHacks.workspaceS: Allow TODOs & Hacks to scan the workspace for comment markers (TODO, FIXME, …). Workspace scans many files—enable when you rely on the Findings Dashboard TODO section.
config.property.todosAndHacks.tags.descr: Tags to search for in comments (case-sensitive in regex).
config.property.todosAndHacks.includeGlo: Glob patterns for files to scan (code and config by default). Add **/*.md to include Markdown docs.
config.property.todosAndHacks.maxFilesTo: Maximum number of files to scan; view shows a message when capped.
config.property.todosAndHacks.autoRefres: Refresh the TODOs & Hacks view when a file is saved (debounced).
config.property.todosAndHacks.groupByTag: Group tree by tag (TODO, FIXME, …) then by file; when off, group by folder then file.
config.property.todosAndHacks.customRege: Optional regex override for matching comment markers. Line-based; use capture group 1 for tag and optional group 2 for snippet. Empty = use default (//, #, <!-- + tags). Invalid regex falls back to default.
config.section.Drift.Advisor.title: Drift Advisor
config.property.driftAdvisor.integration: Enable optional integration with Saropa Drift Advisor. When on, the extension discovers a running Drift Advisor server (ports 8642–8649), fetches index suggestions and anomalies, and shows them in the Drift Advisor view and optionally in Problems.
config.property.driftAdvisor.portRange.d: Port range [min, max] to scan for Drift Advisor server (inclusive).
config.property.driftAdvisor.pollInterva: How often to poll the Drift Advisor server for issues (milliseconds). Set to 0 to disable automatic polling (refresh manually).
config.property.driftAdvisor.showInProbl: Publish Drift Advisor issues as diagnostics in the Problems view (source: Saropa Drift Advisor).
config.section.Package.Vibrancy.title: Package Vibrancy
config.property.packageVibrancy.githubTo: GitHub Personal Access Token for increased API rate limits (60/hr without, 5000/hr with)
config.property.packageVibrancy.scanOnOp: Automatically scan when a Flutter project is opened
config.property.packageVibrancy.autoExpo: Automatically save vibrancy Markdown and JSON reports after each successful scan
config.property.packageVibrancy.includeD: Include dev_dependencies in the vibrancy scan
config.property.packageVibrancy.includeO: Include dependency_overrides (path/git) in the vibrancy scan for security and health checks
config.property.packageVibrancy.cacheTtl: Cache TTL in hours for API responses
config.property.packageVibrancy.startupS: Skip the startup vibrancy scan if the last completed scan finished within this many minutes AND pubspec.lock + scan config are unchanged. Set to 0 to always run the startup scan (no skipping).
config.property.packageVibrancy.showStar: When the startup scan is skipped (cached results reused), show a brief status bar indicator. Leave unset for silent rehydrate.
config.property.packageVibrancy.suppress: Package names to suppress from vibrancy diagnostics and hover (still scanned, shown dimmed in tree)
config.property.packageVibrancy.allowlis: Package names to skip during vibrancy scan (e.g. internal packages)
config.property.packageVibrancy.repoOver: Override GitHub repository URLs for packages where pub.dev data is wrong.
config.property.packageVibrancy.registri: Registry configuration for private Pub servers. Tokens are stored securely in VS Code Secret Storage.
config.property.packageVibrancy.showLock: Show a notification summarizing lock file changes after each scan
config.property.packageVibrancy.showInSt: Show package vibrancy score alongside the lint health score in the status bar
config.property.packageVibrancy.enableCo: Show vibrancy score badges as CodeLens annotations above each dependency in pubspec.yaml
config.property.packageVibrancy.enableAd: Show inline vibrancy badges for new packages being added to pubspec.yaml
config.property.packageVibrancy.codeLens: Controls how much detail the CodeLens annotations show
Score, category, archive size, update status, and alerts
config.property.packageVibrancy.indicato: How to display status indicators in CodeLens
config.property.packageVibrancy.sortSdkF: Keep SDK dependencies (flutter, flutter_test) at the top when sorting
config.property.packageVibrancy.endOfLif: How to display end-of-life package diagnostics in the editor
No diagnostics for end-of-life packages (view in sidebar only)
Show as Warning only when a replacement is known, otherwise Hint
config.property.packageVibrancy.inlineDi: How many Package Vibrancy issues to show as inline diagnostics in pubspec.yaml.
Only critical issues inline: end-of-life, vulnerabilities, family conflicts (recommended)
One diagnostic per package (can be noisy in large pubspecs)
No inline diagnostics — use the Package Vibrancy editor report
Weight for Resolution Velocity in V_score (default 0.5)
Weight for Engagement Level in V_score (default 0.4)
config.property.packageVibrancy.publishe: Max bonus when the dependency's pub.dev publisher is in the trusted list (dart.dev, google.dev, flutter.dev, firebase.google.com). 0 to disable.
config.property.packageVibrancy.upgradeA: Auto-commit each successful upgrade step (creates one commit per package)
config.property.packageVibrancy.upgradeS: Skip flutter test gates during upgrade execution (faster but less safe)
config.property.packageVibrancy.upgradeM: Maximum number of upgrade steps to execute in one run
config.property.packageVibrancy.bulkUpda: Show confirmation dialog before bulk updating dependencies
config.property.packageVibrancy.watchEna: Enable background polling for new package versions
config.property.packageVibrancy.watchInt: How often to check for new package versions (in hours)
config.property.packageVibrancy.watchFil: Filter which packages to watch for new versions
Only watch packages with vibrancy score below 40
Only watch packages specified in watchList
config.property.packageVibrancy.watchLis: Package names to watch for new versions (only used when watchFilter is 'custom')
config.property.packageVibrancy.annotate: Insert decorative section headers above major pubspec sections
config.property.packageVibrancy.treeGrou: How to group packages in the tree view
Flat list sorted by vibrancy score (worst first)
config.property.packageVibrancy.showPrer: Show prerelease versions (dev, beta, rc) in CodeLens and tree view
config.property.packageVibrancy.prerelea: Only show prereleases matching these tags (e.g., ['beta', 'rc']). Empty = show all.
config.property.packageVibrancy.onSaveCh: Command to run when pubspec.yaml is saved with dependency changes. Empty = disabled.
When to trigger the on-save task
Run only when dependency sections change
config.property.packageVibrancy.budget.m: Maximum number of total dependencies allowed (null = no limit)
Maximum total archive size in megabytes (null = no limit)
Minimum average vibrancy score required across all dependencies (null = no limit)
Maximum number of Abandoned packages allowed (null = no limit)
Maximum number of End-of-Life packages allowed (null = no limit)
Maximum number of Outdated packages allowed (null = no limit)
Maximum number of unused dependencies allowed (null = no limit)
config.property.packageVibrancy.enableVu: Query OSV database for known vulnerabilities in dependencies
config.property.packageVibrancy.enableGi: Query GitHub Advisory Database for additional security advisories
config.property.packageVibrancy.enableVe: Fetch PRs and issues between your current version and latest from GitHub. Requires a GitHub token for best results.
config.property.packageVibrancy.vulnSeve: Minimum severity level for displaying vulnerability diagnostics
walkthrough.gettingStarted.title: Getting Started with Saropa Lints
walkthrough.gettingStarted.description: Health Score, Findings dashboard, security matrix, triage, and trends — activity bar plus full-width editor tabs.
walkthrough.step.walkthrough.healthScore:
Your project's quality at a glance: a **0–100 score** in the status bar and Overview view.

Green (80+), yellow (50–79), red (<50). Errors penalize heavily.

[Run analysis](command:saropaLints.runAnalysis) · [Set up project](command:saropaLints.enable)
walkthrough.step.walkthrough.issues.desc:
Findings in the **Findings Dashboard** editor tab, grouped by severity and file. **Error Lens-style** annotations show warnings at the end of each line.

Filter by text, severity, impact, or rule. Right-click to suppress noise.

[Run Analysis](command:saropaLints.runAnalysis)
walkthrough.step.walkthrough.security.de:
**OWASP Top 10** coverage matrix — Mobile and Web. Each category shows violation and rule counts. Click to filter Violations.

Export a compliance report for audits.

[Export OWASP Report](command:saropaLints.exportOwaspReport)
walkthrough.step.walkthrough.triage.desc:
Rules grouped by priority — errors, volume bands, and stylistic. Each group shows **estimated score impact**.

Use **Lints Config** to enable or disable rules; changes write to YAML and re-run analysis.

[Open Lints Config](command:saropaLints.openConfigDashboard)
walkthrough.step.walkthrough.trends.desc:
**File Risk** ranks files by violation density — riskiest first.

The Status panel shows score progression over time. Regression alerts and milestone celebrations keep you on track.

[Show Status panel](command:saropaLints.status.focus)
walkthrough.step.walkthrough.packageVibr:
Scan dependencies for version gaps, maintenance signals, and known issues. Export a **CycloneDX SBOM** for supply-chain audits.

Results appear as CodeLens badges and in the **Package Dashboard** editor tab.

[Scan Package Vibrancy](command:saropaLints.packageVibrancy.scan)
walkthrough.step.walkthrough.todosAndHac:
Find every `TODO`, `HACK`, and `FIXME` comment across the workspace. Group by tag type or folder, and click to jump to the source.

[Refresh TODOs & Hacks](command:saropaLints.todosAndHacks.refresh)
walkthrough.step.walkthrough.crossFile.d:
Run project-wide checks that single-file analysis cannot do: unused files, circular dependencies, import stats, feature dependencies, dead imports, unused symbols, unused l10n keys, duplicate blocks, snapshot JSON, graph export, and HTML report.

[Find Unused Files](command:saropaLints.crossFile.unusedFiles) · [Generate Cross-File HTML Report](command:saropaLints.crossFile.report)
walkthrough.step.walkthrough.commandCata:
115+ commands organized by category with search. Find analysis, security, package health, and reporting commands in one place.

[Browse All Commands](command:saropaLints.showCommandCatalog)
walkthrough.step.walkthrough.aboutSaropa:
Saropa Pty Limited — building digital safeguards since 2010.

Dart packages, VS Code extensions, and consumer apps for developers who value reliability.

[Visit saropa.com](https://saropa.com/)
